steps.title: Reduce the angle using the period of tangent
steps.content:
Since $\tan(\theta + \pi) = \tan(\theta)$, tangent repeats every $\pi$ radians. To reduce $\frac{92\pi}{3}$, divide by $\pi$:

$$\frac{92\pi}{3} \div \pi = \frac{92}{3} = 30\frac{2}{3}$$

This means $\frac{92\pi}{3} = 30\pi + \frac{2\pi}{3}$. Since $30\pi$ represents 30 complete periods, we have:

$$\tan\left(\frac{92\pi}{3}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{2\pi}{3}\right)$$

steps.title: Use reference angle and quadrant to evaluate
steps.content:
The angle $\frac{2\pi}{3}$ is in the second quadrant (between $\frac{\pi}{2}$ and $\pi$). Find the reference angle:

$$\text{Reference angle} = \pi - \frac{2\pi}{3} = \frac{\pi}{3}$$

We know $\tan\left(\frac{\pi}{3}\right) = \sqrt{3}$. In the second quadrant, tangent is negative, so:

$$\tan\left(\frac{2\pi}{3}\right) = -\sqrt{3}$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
